content.full_text:
Fa.rn Polrucal Pnlcucns CouurssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Srreet . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(e 1 6) 3 22f$6dui 
{ Tz:', L1di"',ru
Laurence S. Zakson
Reich, Adell, Crost & Cvitan
501 Shatto Place, Suite 100
Los Angeles, CA 90020-1792
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. .4.-00-182
Dear Mr. Zakson:
This lettbr is a response to your request foradvice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTION
What is the appropriate way for the Laborers International Union of North
America, Local Union 300, AFL-CIO (Union) to report the costs associated with the use
of a telephone bank maintained by the Union on a year-round basis when the phone bank
is used to make independent expenditures?
CONCLUSION
Only additional costs associated with the use of the phones in the making of an
independent expenditure are required to be reported on Schedule E of the.Form 460. To
the extent that the telephone lines were in existence prior to the making of an independent
expenditure, costs to maintain the lines are not reportable.
FACTS
The Union maintains a bank of telephones on a year-round basis. These
telephones are used only sporadically and, then, substantially all of the time for political
purposes. The overwhelmingly preponderant political purpose for which these
telephones are used is independent expenditures on behalf of selected candidates for
office and in support of or opposition to ballot measures.
' 
Gouerffnent Code sections 8 1000 - 9 10 14. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections
18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


Laurence S. Zakson
August 22,2000
A-00-182
There are only minimal incremental costs associated with the use of the
telephones to make the calls reported as independent expenditures.
ANALYSIS
Committees are required to report all expenditures made. When an expenditure is
determined to be an independent expenditure, the filer must calculate the costs associated
with making the independent expenditure in order to report properly. (Section 84211.)
Section 82031 defines an independent expenditure as:
"...an expenditure made by any person in connection with a
communication which expressly advocates the election or defeat of
a clearly identified candidate or the qualification, passage or defeat
of a clearly identified measure, or taken as a whole and in context,
unambiguously urges a particular result in an election but which is
not made to or at the behest of the affected candidate or committee."
Regulation 18225.7 states that "made at the behest of'means:
"...made under the control or at the direction of, in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with, at the request or suggestion
of, or with the express, prior consent of. Such iurangement must occur
prior to the making of a communication described in Government Code
Section 8203I;'
A payment that is made at the behest of a candidate or other committee is a
nonmonetary, or in-kind, contribution. The value of an in-kind contribution is the fair
market value of the goods or services donated. The fair market value is whatever it
would cost the candidate or committee to obtain the same or similar goods or services on
the open market. (Miller Advice Letter, No. I-96-243.) However, if the payment is an
independent expenditure, the amount of the reportable expenditure is the costs
attributable to the communication. (Regulation 18225(b)(3)(A).) Under your fact
pattern, only the additional costs of using the phone banks expressly to advocate on
behalf of a particular candidate, or in support of or opposition to a ballot measure, are
reportable.


Laurence S. Zakson
August 22,2000
A-00-182
If you have additional questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
PhD
Political Reform Consultant II

CONCLUSION: Only additional costs associated with the use of the phones in the making of an
